final_conclusion: M04/M05 exhibits a small negative-harm signature replicable across TabM, ModernNCA, and TabR. Preregistered interventions targeting weak-signal dilution (H2), finite-sample variance (H3), and early-stopping effects (H4) did not support any causal mechanism at the sentinel level. The phenomenon is robust observationally but mechanistically unexplained.

claims.CL13a: PARTIALLY_CONFIRMED (cross-model directional replication established, TabR CI crosses zero)
claims.CL13b: NOT_CREATED (no hypothesis passed sentinel promotion gate)

interpretation: high-quality null result — rules out TabM-specific anomaly and three most natural explanations
recommended_wording: Temporal structured mechanisms M04 and M05 exhibit a small negative-harm signature across TabM, ModernNCA, and TabR. However, preregistered interventions targeting weak-signal dilution, finite-sample variance, and early-stopping effects did not explain the phenomenon. The recurrence establishes that the effect is not unique to TabM, but the failed sentinel interventions leave its causal mechanism unresolved.
